state.topic: Surprisingly, mixing vinegar and baking soda doesn’t just fizz; it produces a gas that can actually put out a small flame—shown in class 10 chemistry.
state.script: Mixing vinegar and baking soda does much more than just create a fun little fizz. When you combine these two common kitchen staples, the acetic acid reacts with the sodium bicarbonate to rapidly release invisible carbon dioxide bubbles. You will notice the mixture bubbling excitedly as this heavy gas accumulates around your workspace. That same invisible cloud is actually denser than our everyday atmosphere, so it naturally sinks downward just like an invisible blanket. If you carefully tilt that container over a small lit candle, the carbon dioxide will quickly displace the oxygen and instantly extinguish the fire. You have just transformed a messy countertop reaction into a brilliant classroom demonstration of real fire safety chemistry.
state.caption: Kitchen chemistry magic 🔬🕯️ #Class10Chemistry
